answer.metric_shift: context_relevance improved from 0.22 in trace-003 to 0.91 in trace-004
answer.safe_evidence_boundary: Submit redacted trace IDs, scores, and short diagnoses, with no secrets or private user data.
answer.next_action: remove the leaking loan feature, retrain the loan model, add test coverage for weather-refund-policy, and monitor context_relevance

evidence: This incident has two evidence threads that must not be blended. On the model side, case C-104 was predicted as late_repayment_risk even though the actual label was paid_on_time. SHAP shows late_payments and credit_utilization pushing risk up, while stable_income and employment_months push risk down. The feature audit also flags post_approval_call_count as leakage because it is only known after the decision. On the LLM app side, trace-003 failed because retrieval returned venue Wi-Fi context for a typhoon refund question, producing context_relevance 0.22. The fixed trace-004 used event-policy context and raised context_relevance to 0.91. The safe public evidence is redacted IDs, scores, and diagnosis text, not secrets. The action plan is to remove and retrain the leaking model feature, keep weather-refund-policy as a regression dataset item, and monitor retrieval quality.
